context: Tests two interacting tile-prescribed rules at triage time: (1) the rubric's 'Content-equivalent answers' rule â€” a YAML form field counts as present when the substance is supplied even if the author used different headings; and (2) the rubric's vocabulary rule â€” 'Always use the word `template`, never `form`' when describing the artefact to a contributor in suggested comments. The fixture has a YAML form with required fields `version`, `what-happened`, `expected`, `repro` (optional `logs`); the body uses freeform `## Description / ## Environment / ## Reproduction / ## Expected / ## Logs` headings whose substance covers every required field. Tile-prescribed outcome: `Matches well enough` because every required field's substance is present. The suggested comment is `No comment needed` (or, at most, an optional manual-check note observing that the author wrote freeform markdown â€” note only; the rubric's content-equivalence rule says the body still satisfies the template's request when the substance is there, regardless of whether GitHub renders the issue body as a form-shaped artefact). Two baseline failure modes this catches: (a) grading the body as `Significant deviation` because the headings don't match `id` / `label` verbatim, and (b) drafting a comment that asks the contributor to 'fill out the form' or 'use the form fields' â€” both wrong. Tile-specific values: the `Matches well enough` bucket choice on a non-verbatim body, the literal word `template` in any prose about the artefact, and the absence of an over-asking comment.

checklist.name: Reads the YAML form fields by id / label
checklist.description: The agent identifies the form's declared fields (`version`, `what-happened`, `expected`, `repro`, `logs`) and treats them as the source of truth for what the body needs to answer. Tile-specific: the rubric's YAML form rule 'For YAML issue forms, map fields by `id`, `label`, and `validations.required`'; baseline agents either ignore the YAML structure or map fields by ad-hoc keyword matching.

checklist.name: Recognizes content-equivalent answers across renamed headings
checklist.description: The agent maps the body's freeform headings to the form's required fields by substance â€” `## Description` â†’ `what-happened`, `## Environment` â†’ `version` (the version is `2.4.1`), `## Reproduction` â†’ `repro`, `## Expected` â†’ `expected`. It does NOT flag any of these as missing on the basis that the headings differ from the form's labels. Tile-specific: the rubric's 'Content-equivalent answers' rule explicitly authorises this and is the load-bearing tile signal here.

checklist.name: Classifies as Matches well enough, not Significant deviation
checklist.description: Because every required field's substance is present, the result bucket is `Matches well enough` (or equivalent â€” 'body answers what the template asks for', 'no rewrite needed'). `Slight deviation` is partial credit only if it doesn't ask for a rewrite and the only flagged item is 'wrote freeform instead of form fields' as a stylistic note. `Significant deviation` is incorrect.

checklist.name: Does not draft a 'fill out the form' comment
checklist.description: The output does not contain a suggested comment asking the contributor to 'fill out the form fields', 'use the form template', 'rewrite using the YAML form', or any equivalent. The body's substance is already present; asking for a rewrite is template invention. The tile-prescribed answer is `No comment needed` or a tentative manual-check snippet. Tile-specific: this is the failure mode the content-equivalence rule prevents.

checklist.name: Says `template`, never `form`, in prose to the contributor
checklist.description: Wherever the agent's output describes the artefact in prose intended for the user (or as a suggested comment to the contributor), it uses the word `template` and not `form`. References to YAML 'form' as a structural type (e.g. 'this is a YAML issue form') in the agent's analysis are fine; the rule is about the user-facing wording. Tile-specific: the rubric's explicit vocabulary rule 'Do not call the template a `form` in suggested comments; use `template`'.

checklist.name: Does not ask for the optional `logs` field
checklist.description: The body includes a `## Logs / extra context` section with a sample log line, satisfying the optional `logs` field anyway. The agent does NOT ask the contributor to add logs as a missing field. Tile-specific: the rubric's optional-vs-required distinction; baseline agents often ask for any field present in the template regardless of `validations: required`.
